name: Wheat-Flour Applesauce Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour, wheat flour, baking powder, soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, and mace on a plate.
2. Cream together the shortening, sugar, eggs, and vanilla until smooth.
3. Add the applesauce and buttermilk to the creamed mixture and stir well.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing after each addition until smooth.
5. Refrigerate the dough for 2 hours.
6. Preheat oil in a deep fryer or pot to 375°F (190°C).
7. Dust a floured cutting board with flour and cut dough into 2 1/2-inch rounds using a floured cutter.
8. Gently remove the center of each round, leaving a hole.
9. Fry the doughnuts in batches until golden brown on both sides.
10. Drain the fried doughnuts on paper towels and sprinkle with sugar.
11. Makes approximately 4 to 4 1/2 dozen doughnuts.
